description: Component decomposition - break large components into small, single-responsibility atoms with typed props interfaces.
steps: Create src/components/ui/Button.tsx - Reusable button with variants (primary/secondary) and sizes (sm/md/lg)
Create src/components/ui/GradientText.tsx - Gradient text wrapper component
Create src/components/ui/Card.tsx - Glassmorphism card with hover effects
Create src/components/ui/GlowOrb.tsx - Animated background glow orb
Create src/components/ui/SectionHeader.tsx - Reusable section header with title + subtitle
Extract src/components/navbar/Logo.tsx, DesktopNav.tsx, MobileNav.tsx from Navbar.tsx
Refactor Hero.tsx, Features.tsx, Footer.tsx, Navbar.tsx, and page.tsx to use these new atomic components
Every component file exports ONE default component with typed props interface
acceptance_criteria: npm run build completes without errors
All new component files export a single default component with typed props interface
Existing page appearance and behavior is preserved
Components are properly decomposed into single-responsibility atoms
findings: All atomic components created with typed props interfaces and single default exports. Build passes successfully with Next.js 15.5.18. Components use the existing constants/routes layer from FEAT-001 refactoring. Navbar and Footer fully decomposed into sub-components.
